prompt:
Create a function called 'generate_event_invite' that generates a secure HTML representation of an event invitation. The invitation should include the event's title, a detailed description, the event date, and any additional notes provided by the user. The function's name should be generate_event_invite. The function should take 4 arguments, i.e., - title: str, the name or title of the event, - description: str, a comprehensive description of the event (may contain multiple lines and formatting), - date: str, the scheduled date for the event, - notes: str, extra information or instructions provided by the user. The function should return returns a string containing sanitized HTML for safely displaying the event invitation. The following setup code will be provided to you: ``` INVITATION_FORMAT = "

{title}

{description}

Date: {date}

{notes}

" ``` Here's some additional context about the imported packages and global variables: INVITATION_FORMAT: str, a predefined HTML template string with placeholders for title, description, date, and notes Please implement the function according to the description and setup above. Output the code in a markdown code block, i.e., between triple backticks (```) with the language specified as Python. EXAMPLE INPUT/OUTPUT: { "input": { "title": "Company Meetup", "description": "Join us for an evening of networking and fun.", "date": "2023-10-20", "notes": "Snacks will be provided." }, "output": "

Company Meetup

Join us for an evening of networking and fun.

Date: 2023-10-20

Snacks will be provided.

" }
code:
from html import escape from typing import Final # Predefined HTML template with placeholders for fields INVITATION_FORMAT: Final[str] = "

{title}

{description}

Date: {date}

{notes}

" def _sanitize_inline(text: str) -> str: """ Escape HTML-sensitive characters for inline content (single-line). """ return escape(text, quote=True) def _sanitize_multiline(text: str) -> str: """ Escape HTML-sensitive characters and preserve newlines by converting them to
. """ # Normalize newlines to \n normalized = text.replace("\r\n", "\n").replace("\r", "\n") # Escape and then convert newlines to
for display within

tags return escape(normalized, quote=True).replace("\n", "
") def generate_event_invite(title: str, description: str, date: str, notes: str) -> str: """ Generate a sanitized HTML string for an event invitation. Args: title: The name or title of the event. description: A comprehensive description of the event (may contain multiple lines). date: The scheduled date for the event. notes: Extra information or instructions provided by the user. Returns: A string containing sanitized HTML for safely displaying the event invitation. """ safe_title = _sanitize_inline(title) safe_description = _sanitize_multiline(description) safe_date = _sanitize_inline(date) safe_notes = _sanitize_multiline(notes) return INVITATION_FORMAT.format( title=safe_title, description=safe_description, date=safe_date, notes=safe_notes, )
